channel.title: Indian Defence Research Wing
channel.description: INDIAN DEFENCE NEWS, LATEST DEFENCE NEWS, INDIAN ARMY, INDIAN AIR FORCE, INDIAN NAVY, DEFENSE NEWS

item.title: India Achieves STANAG Level 5 Protection on Vikram VT-21 Through Indigenous Composite Hull Technology
item.description: In a major milestone for India’s armoured vehicle development, the Vikram VT-21 tracked platform has successfully achieved STANAG Level 5 ballistic protection using a fully indigenous composite hull, marking a significant leap in lightweight, high-performance armour technology.
item.dc:creator: IDRW.ORG

item.title: IAF’s Mehar Baba Competition Targets Swarm-Based “Distributed Radar” to Redefine Air Surveillance
item.description: The Indian Air Force’s Mehar Baba Competition (MBC) is pivoting toward one of the most disruptive concepts in modern air warfare: swarm-enabled unmanned aerial systems functioning as a distributed airborne radar. This year’s edition is focused on developing a proof-of-concept where multiple UAS collaborate autonomously to detect, track, and relay aerial targets in contested environments, effectively acting as a networked sensor grid rather than isolated drones.
item.dc:creator: IDRW.ORG

item.title: India’s Fourth S-400 Squadron Seals Western Air Defence: No Coverage Gaps for PAF to Exploit, Cementing Its Worst Nightmare After Operation Sindoor Losses
item.description: The imminent arrival of India’s fourth S-400 squadron, expected by May 2026 to coincide with the first anniversary of Operation Sindoor, marks a decisive enhancement to the Indian Air Force’s layered air defence network along the western frontier. Strategically positioned in the sector between Punjab and Gujarat—bridging the existing deployments in the Pathankot-Punjab region and the Rajasthan-Gujarat corridor—this new formation will eliminate the last remaining vulnerabilities in coverage, creating an unbroken, overlapping “Sudarshan Chakra” shield that spans the entire Indo-Pakistan border. For the Pakistan Air Force, already reeling from the demonstrated lethality of the S-400 during last year’s clashes, the development represents an operational nightmare with no safe corridors left to penetrate.
item.dc:creator: AFI

item.title: India’s CADET Air Defence Push: 83 Tracked Systems to Strengthen Networked Warfare Under Akashteer Framework
item.description: India’s Ministry of Defence has moved forward with the procurement of 83 Carrier Air Defence Tracked (CADET) systems, marking a decisive doctrinal shift toward highly mobile, network-centric air defence for mechanised formations. The program reflects the Indian Army’s evolving threat perception, where traditional aerial threats are now complemented by a rapidly expanding spectrum of low-flying, low-RCS targets such as drones, loitering munitions, and attack helicopters operating in contested battlefield environments.
item.dc:creator: AFI

item.title: China Wary as India Expands Defence Ties with South Korea in Artillery and Air Defence Systems
item.description: China is closely watching India’s growing defence-industrial cooperation with South Korea, with analysts suggesting Beijing views the development as “inevitably sensitive” given the ongoing border dispute between the two Asian powers. The assessment, reported by the Hong Kong-based South China Morning Post, comes as New Delhi and Seoul move to deepen collaboration in artillery, air defence systems, and advanced military technologies.
item.dc:creator: AFI

item.title: Can India’s Aging Jaguar Fleet Be Replaced by the Indigenous Super Su-30MKI?
item.description: As the Indian Air Force grapples with squadron shortfalls and an aging inventory, a pressing question looms over its strike capabilities: can the entire fleet of over 115 SEPECAT Jaguars be phased out and replaced by HAL’s upgraded Super Su-30MKI fighters? With the Jaguars now pushing 45 years in service and showing signs of fatigue—from engine derating in summer heat to a string of crashes in 2025—the idea of consolidating around a single, modernized heavy multirole platform is gaining traction. Yet, the transition would demand not just new aircraft but a rethinking of doctrines built around the Jaguar’s unique low-level strike niche.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: A Fleet Built Around 400 Super-30MKI Fighters: The Case for a Unified, Fully Sovereign Combat Aircraft Ecosystem
item.description: The Indian Air Force is approaching a decisive transition in its long-term force structure. Over the next decade, as the Jaguar, Mirage 2000, and MiG-29 fleets retire, the opportunity emerges to consolidate the combat inventory around a single heavy-class multi-role platform: an upgraded Su-30MKI, or the Super-30MKI standard. Such consolidation offers substantial efficiency gains across logistics, training, sustainment, and mission integration, while laying the foundation for greater technological sovereignty.
item.dc:creator: admin

item.title: Cong Accuses Rajnath Singh Of Giving ‘Shameful Clean Chit’ To Pak For Saying Terror Has ‘No Nationality’ At SCO
item.description: This comes after ‘RMO India’ took to social media ‘X’ and said, “It was a testimony to our zero-tolerance outlook towards terrorism and its perpetrators, which was endorsed by this esteemed Forum. But the real test of our collective credibility remains in consistency. We must not forget that terrorism has no nationality and no theology. No grievance, real or supposed, can become an excuse for terrorism and humanitarian loss: Raksha Mantri Shri Rajnath Singh.”
item.dc:creator: IANS

item.title: China fighter jet giant’s sales surge after India-Pakistan clash
item.description: Revenue rose 15.8% to 75.4 billion yuan ($11 billion) in 2025, with profit up 6.5% to 3.4 billion yuan, the jetmaker said in a statement Tuesday, April 28, night. Both are the highest-ever for the Chengdu-based company, according to data compiled by Bloomberg. First-quarter sales rose almost 80% on year, it added.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg

item.title: CDS General Chauhan’s Visit To UK Marks Landmark Moment In Defence Partnership: British High Commission
item.description: UK Chief of the Defence Staff Air Chief Marshal Sir Richard Knighton hosted General Chauhan during the three-day visit, which included bilateral talks, ministerial engagement and defence industry collaboration. “General Anil Chauhan, CDS, during his official visit to the United Kingdom interacted with Air Chief Marshal Sir Richard Knighton, CDS UK. India and the UK reaffirmed their shared resolve to collaboratively address contemporary challenges from global trade imbalances to emerging cyber threats while advancing a peaceful and prosperous Indo-Pacific and harnessing accelerated technological transformation,” Headquarters of Integrated Defence Staff posted on X.
item.dc:creator: IANS

item.title: Mira Road Stabbing: ATS Probes Alleged ISIS Links, Handwritten Note Surfaces
item.description: According to sources, in a purported handwritten letter recovered from Zeab Zubair Ansari’s home, the accused, allegedly wrote, “I gave bait to ISIS.” The note also reportedly contained a hand-drawn ISIS flag along with several extremist statements, including warnings such as “Lone wolves will pounce on you” and references to “jihad in Bilad al-Hind.”
item.dc:creator: IANS

item.title: Union Minister Ravneet Singh Visits Blast Site In Punjab, Reviews Security
item.description: Later, talking to the media, the minister expressed serious concern over the repeated incidents targeting railway infrastructure in the state and assured strict and immediate measures to strengthen safety and monitoring.
item.dc:creator: IANS

item.title: Punjab Police Install 2,291 CCTV Cameras Along Pakistan Border
item.description: Under “Gangstran Te Vaar”, the police are strengthening the technology-driven security grid designed to choke the very lifelines of organised crime networks operating with cross-border linkages. While the Border Security Force (BSF) continues to guard the international boundary, the police are ensuring that even if contraband slips through, it doesn’t travel far.
item.dc:creator: IANS

item.title: Union Minister Emphasises Close Coordination For Border Security And Welfare In Mizoram
item.description: A Mizoram government official stated that the visiting Minister stressed enhanced cooperation between Assam Rifles, state police, district authorities, and Village Councils to strengthen security, promote development, and improve the welfare of people living in border villages.
item.dc:creator: IANS

item.title: Encounter Breaks Out Between Maoists And Security Forces In Jharkhand’s West Singhbhum Forests
item.description: According to information, security forces have been conducting an intensive search operation for the past two days in the Boroi and Toonbeda areas under the Goelkera police station limits.
item.dc:creator: IANS
